onboarding.subtitle: Build real products in 7 days. AI-guided, step by step.
onboarding.question: What's your goal?
onboarding.goal_money_desc: Build products that generate revenue fast
onboarding.goal_startup_desc: Build and ship a real SaaS or product
onboarding.goal_ai_desc: Learn to build with AI as your co-pilot

dashboard.builder_desc: Got an idea? Get a step-by-step build plan from AI

mission.complete_day1_desc: Most people never get past 'thinking about it.' You just defined a real product. +50 XP bonus

mentor.title: AI Mentor
mentor.greeting_sub: Ask me anything about the current step — I'll help you build it.
mentor.quick_help_step: What should I do for this step? Guide me.
mentor.quick_generate: Generate something I can use right now for this step — a message, an offer, or a template.
mentor.quick_stuck: I'm stuck and don't know what to do. Help me take the next small step.

leads.title: Your Leads
leads.empty: No leads yet. Complete "Send to 3 people" to start your pipeline.

skills.title: Your Strengths
skills.empty: Complete steps to discover your strengths.

step_modal.ai_hint: Use the AI Mentor below to complete this step, then come back and confirm.
step_modal.confirm_hint: Confirm that you've completed this action before checking it off.

side_quests.title: Side Quests
side_quests.day1_sq1: Research 3 competitors and list what they're missing
side_quests.day1_sq2: Ask AI to find trending problems in your niche
side_quests.day2_sq1: Add a FAQ section to your landing page
side_quests.day2_sq2: Ask AI to critique your landing page design
side_quests.day3_sq2: Set up a referral incentive for early users
side_quests.day4_sq1: Write a social post about your outreach experience
side_quests.day4_sq2: Send to 2 more people beyond the 3
side_quests.day5_sq1: Create a short testimonial request template
side_quests.day5_sq2: Get 1 warm intro from an existing contact
side_quests.day6_sq1: Build a workflow that auto-responds to new leads
side_quests.day6_sq2: Document your automation setup for future reference
side_quests.day7_sq1: Create a short demo video of your product
side_quests.day7_sq2: Reach out to 1 influencer or community leader

levels.certificate_subtitle: has successfully completed the 7-day VibeCoding program
levels.enter_name: Enter your name for the certificate:

missions.day1_desc: Today you discover what to build. You'll identify a real problem, define your audience, and craft a clear offer. By the end, you'll know exactly what you're building and for whom.
missions.day1_step1_desc: Think about problems you or people around you face daily. What do people complain about? What tasks are annoying, slow, or expensive? Pick one real problem.
missions.day1_step2_desc: Who has this problem the most? List 3 specific groups of people. Be specific — 'busy freelancers' is better than 'everyone'.
missions.day1_step3_desc: How would you solve this problem? Write a simple description of your product or service. Don't overthink it — a simple solution is better than a complex one.
missions.day1_step4_desc: Use the AI Mentor to analyze your idea. It will help you identify strengths, weaknesses, and potential improvements.
missions.day1_step5_desc: Craft one clear sentence: 'I help [who] do [what] so they can [result].' This is your elevator pitch. Make it impossible to misunderstand.
missions.day1_step6_desc: Open your contacts, social media, or communities. Find 5 real people who have the problem you solve. You'll reach out to them later.
missions.day2_desc: Yesterday you found your idea. Today you build something real — a landing page, a simple tool, or a service page. By the end, you'll have something you can show to people.
missions.day2_step1_desc: List the absolute minimum your product needs to work. What's the ONE thing it must do? Strip away everything else.
missions.day2_step2_desc: Pick the fastest way to build: Carrd, Notion, Framer, a simple HTML page, or a no-code tool. Speed beats perfection.
missions.day2_step3_desc: Build a one-page site with: your offer (from Day 1), how it works (3 steps), and a clear CTA button. Keep it simple and honest.
missions.day2_step4_desc: Use the AI Mentor to write your headline, description, and CTA text. Share your one-sentence offer and let AI polish it.
missions.day2_step5_desc: Publish your landing page or tool. Get a real URL you can share with people. It doesn't have to be perfect — it has to be live.
missions.day2_step6_desc: Open your page in a new browser tab as if you've never seen it. Is the offer clear? Does the CTA work? Would you sign up?
missions.day2_step7_desc: Fix any issues you found in testing. Broken links, confusing copy, missing CTA — address them now before sharing with real people.
missions.day3_desc: You have a product. Now set up the infrastructure — payments, booking, and communication. By the end, someone can find you, sign up, and pay.
missions.day3_step1_desc: Create a way for people to sign up or book — Google Form, Calendly, Typeform, or a simple email link. Pick one and make it live.
missions.day3_step2_desc: Decide on a price. Start simple — one tier, one price. You can always add tiers later. Write down what's included.
missions.day3_step3_desc: Set up a payment method — Stripe payment link, PayPal.me, or bank transfer. Connect it to your landing page or booking flow.
missions.day3_step4_desc: Choose how clients will reach you — WhatsApp Business, Telegram, email, or a form. Add the contact method to your landing page.
missions.day3_step5_desc: Go through the entire journey: landing page → signup → payment → communication. Does everything connect? Fix any gaps.
missions.day3_step6_desc: Confirm: your landing page links to booking, booking connects to payment, and clients can contact you. Your system is ready for real users.
missions.day4_desc: You've built something real. Now it's time to reach out. Today you contact real people and get your first client or user. You have a product, a system, and an offer — you're ready.
missions.day4_step1_desc: Open your contacts, Twitter, LinkedIn, or Telegram. Find 5 real people who have the problem you solve. Prioritize people you already know.
missions.day4_step2_desc: Use the AI Mentor to craft a short, personal DM. No spam — lead with value, not a pitch. Reference their specific situation.
missions.day4_step3_desc: Ask the AI Mentor to help you write a compelling pitch — something you can send alongside your link. Include your offer, proof, and CTA.
missions.day4_step4_desc: Pick 3 from your list and hit send. You have your message, your product, and your system — everything is ready. The first message is the hardest.
missions.day4_step5_desc: If someone replies — great, keep the conversation going. If not, that's normal. Plan to follow up tomorrow.
missions.day4_step6_desc: Write down what happened. Any replies? Any interest? What would you change? This is your first real sales data.
missions.day5_desc: You've made your first contacts. Now scale it — more messages, better messages, real traction. Today you turn outreach into a repeatable process.
missions.day5_step1_desc: Look at Day 4 results. Which messages got replies? Which people were most interested? Write down the pattern.
missions.day5_step2_desc: Share your best message with the AI Mentor. Ask it to make it sharper based on what worked. Get 2-3 variations.
missions.day5_step3_desc: Write a short post about what you're building or a tip related to your offer. Post it on your main channel. This builds trust passively.
missions.day5_step4_desc: Pick the one platform where your audience lives — Telegram, Instagram, LinkedIn, or Twitter. Focus your energy there.
missions.day5_step5_desc: Use your improved message. Send to 3 new people — personalize the first line for each person. Quality over quantity.
missions.day5_step6_desc: Write a follow-up message for people who didn't reply. Or create a second content post. Keep your presence active.
missions.day5_step7_desc: Log every reply: interested, not interested, no response. Count your numbers. This is your conversion data.
missions.day6_desc: You have clients and a system. Now make it run without you. Today you add AI-powered automation to save hours of manual work.
missions.day6_step1_desc: What do you do manually over and over? Responding to inquiries, sending follow-ups, creating content? List the tasks that eat your time.
missions.day6_step2_desc: Choose your tool — n8n, Make, Zapier, or a simple script. Create an account and explore the basics.
missions.day6_step3_desc: Create an automation: when someone signs up or fills your form → you get a notification on Telegram, email, or Slack. Never miss a lead.
missions.day6_step4_desc: Set up auto-generation of content — social posts, email responses, or follow-up messages. Describe what you want to automate.
missions.day6_step5_desc: Trigger each automation and verify it works end to end. Fix any broken connections. Your system should run on autopilot.
missions.day7_desc: Everything is built, connected, and automated. Today you go public. Launch your product, gather feedback, and plan for growth.
missions.day7_step1_desc: Use the AI Mentor to draft a compelling launch announcement. Include what you built, who it's for, and a link to try it.
missions.day7_step2_desc: Share your launch on Twitter, LinkedIn, and one community (Reddit, Indie Hackers, Product Hunt, or a Telegram group).
missions.day7_step3_desc: Add tracking to your site — PostHog, Plausible, or Google Analytics. Know how many people visit and what they click.
missions.day7_step4_desc: Add a feedback form or reach out to your first users. What do they love? What's confusing? What would they pay more for?
missions.day7_step5_desc: Based on 7 days of data and feedback, plan your next moves. What to improve, what to double down on, what to drop.
missions.day1_step1_prompt: What problem do you want to solve?
missions.day1_step2_prompt: List 3 groups of people who have this problem:
missions.day1_step2_placeholder: e.g. Busy freelancers, small business owners...
missions.day1_step4_prompt: Ask the AI Mentor to validate your idea. What did it say?
missions.day1_step5_placeholder: I help [who] do [what] so they can [result]
missions.day2_step1_prompt: What are the must-have features of your MVP?
missions.day2_step1_placeholder: The ONE thing it must do is...
missions.day2_step2_prompt: Which tool will you use to build?
missions.day2_step3_prompt: Paste your landing page content or link:
missions.day2_step4_prompt: Use the AI Mentor to generate your copy. Got it?
missions.day2_step6_prompt: Did you test the page as a user? Any issues?
missions.day2_step7_prompt: Did you fix all the issues found?
missions.day3_step1_prompt: What booking/signup method did you set up?
missions.day3_step4_prompt: What communication channel did you choose?
missions.day3_step5_prompt: Did you test the full flow end to end?
missions.day3_step6_prompt: Is everything connected? Landing → signup → payment → contact?
missions.day4_step2_prompt: Use the AI Mentor to craft your outreach message. Ready?
missions.day4_step3_prompt: Ask the AI Mentor to create your pitch. Got it?
missions.day4_step4_prompt: Who did you send to? (3 people)
missions.day4_step5_prompt: Did you follow up or handle replies?
missions.day4_step6_placeholder: Any replies? Interest? What would you change?
missions.day5_step1_prompt: What pattern did you notice from Day 4?
missions.day5_step2_prompt: Ask the AI Mentor to improve your best message. Got variations?
missions.day5_step3_prompt: Paste your content post (or describe it):
missions.day5_step4_prompt: Which platform are you focusing on?
missions.day5_step5_prompt: Who did you send to? (3 people)
missions.day5_step6_prompt: What follow-up content did you create?
missions.day5_step7_prompt: What are your numbers? (interested / no reply / not interested)
missions.day5_step7_placeholder: e.g. 3 interested, 2 no reply, 1 not interested
missions.day6_step1_prompt: What tasks do you want to automate?
missions.day6_step1_placeholder: e.g. Responding to inquiries, sending follow-ups...
missions.day6_step2_prompt: Is your automation tool set up?
missions.day6_step5_prompt: Are all automations tested and working?
missions.day7_step1_prompt: Use the AI Mentor to draft your launch post. Ready to publish?
missions.day7_step2_prompt: Which 3 platforms did you post on?
missions.day7_step3_prompt: Is analytics tracking live on your site?
missions.day7_step4_prompt: Do you have a way to collect user feedback?
missions.day7_step5_prompt: What are your top 3 priorities for next week?

learning.title: Learning
learning.subtitle: Learn only what you need, when you need it. Each lesson is linked to your missions.
learning.cta_desc: Short lessons linked to your missions
learning.basics_what_is_ai: What is AI and how to use it
learning.basics_what_is_ai_exp: AI (Artificial Intelligence) is software that can understand text, generate content, and make decisions. Tools like ChatGPT and Claude are 'large language models' — they predict the best next word based on your input. Think of them as a very smart intern: fast, knowledgeable, but needs clear instructions.
learning.basics_what_is_ai_ex: You type: 'Give me 5 business ideas for busy moms.' AI instantly generates ideas based on patterns from millions of texts — faster than any Google search.
learning.basics_what_is_ai_tip: Open your AI Mentor right now and ask: 'What are 3 problems people pay to solve in [your niche]?' Use the answer in Day 1.
learning.basics_mvp: MVP mindset: build less, learn faster
learning.basics_mvp_exp: MVP (Minimum Viable Product) means building the smallest possible version of your idea that still works. The goal isn't perfection — it's speed. Ship something in hours, not weeks. Real feedback from real users is worth more than months of planning.
learning.basics_mvp_ex: Dropbox started as a 3-minute video showing the concept. No product built yet. The video got 75,000 signups overnight — proving demand before writing a single line of code.
learning.basics_mvp_tip: For your Day 2 MVP: pick ONE feature, build it in under 2 hours, and share the link with one person today.
learning.basics_landing_exp: A landing page has one job: turn a visitor into a lead or customer. The formula is simple — headline (what you offer), subheadline (why it matters), 3 benefits, social proof, and one clear call-to-action button. No navigation menu, no distractions.
learning.basics_landing_ex: Bad: 'Welcome to our innovative platform.' Good: 'Get 3x more clients from Instagram — without posting every day. Join 500+ freelancers already using it.'
learning.basics_landing_tip: Use this headline formula: '[Desired result] — without [pain point]. [Social proof].' Paste it into your Day 2 landing page.
learning.basics_outreach_exp: Cold outreach works when you lead with value, not a pitch. The formula: personalized opener (show you know them), value statement (what's in it for them), soft CTA (ask a question, not a sale). Keep it under 4 sentences. Nobody reads long DMs from strangers.
learning.basics_outreach_ex: Bad: 'Hi, I have a great product...' Good: 'Hey Sarah, saw your post about struggling with Instagram scheduling. I built a tool that auto-schedules posts — would a free week help?'
learning.basics_outreach_tip: Write your first DM using this template: 'Hey [name], saw your [specific thing]. I [what you do] — would [specific offer] be useful for you?'
learning.basics_automation_exp: Automation means making software do repetitive tasks for you. Tools like n8n, Make, and Zapier connect apps together: when X happens, do Y. No coding needed. The goal: remove yourself from tasks that don't need your brain — follow-ups, notifications, data entry.
learning.basics_automation_ex: Automation: When someone fills your form (Typeform) → send a Telegram notification to you → add them to a Google Sheet → send them a welcome email. All in 5 minutes, no code.
learning.basics_automation_tip: Pick ONE thing you do manually every day. Go to make.com and see if there's a template for it. Set it up during Day 6.
learning.models_comparison_exp: ChatGPT (OpenAI) is best for general tasks and code. Claude (Anthropic) excels at long texts, nuanced reasoning, and following complex instructions. Gemini (Google) is strong at research and summarization. For building a business, you don't need to pick one — use whichever is best for each task.
learning.models_comparison_ex: Need a sales DM? ChatGPT writes punchy copy. Need to analyze your business plan for flaws? Claude gives deeper, more honest feedback. Need market research? Gemini searches the web.
learning.models_comparison_tip: For your 7D(AI)S journey, use the built-in AI Mentor (powered by Claude). For extra tasks, keep ChatGPT open in another tab.
learning.models_when: When to use AI (and when not to)
learning.models_when_exp: Use AI for: generating ideas, writing first drafts, analyzing data, coding, creating templates, and brainstorming. Don't use AI for: final decisions, sensitive personal communication, anything requiring real-world verification, or replacing your unique voice entirely.
learning.models_when_ex: Good use: 'Generate 10 taglines for my fitness coaching service.' Bad use: 'Should I quit my job to start this business?' (AI can help you think through it, but the decision is yours.)
learning.models_when_tip: Rule of thumb: use AI for the FIRST 80% (draft, structure, ideas), then add your personal touch for the last 20%.
learning.models_content_exp: AI can write blog posts, social media content, emails, ad copy, and scripts in seconds. The key is giving it context: who's the audience, what's the tone, what's the goal. The better your input, the better the output. Always edit AI content — add your personality and real examples.
learning.models_content_ex: Prompt: 'Write a LinkedIn post about launching my MVP. Tone: excited but honest. Audience: indie hackers. Include a specific lesson I learned.' → AI generates a post you can tweak and publish in 2 minutes.
learning.models_content_tip: Create a 'content brief' for AI: Audience + Tone + Goal + Key point. Save it as a template. Use it every time you need content for Days 5 and 7.
learning.models_automation_exp: Combine AI with automation tools for superpowers. Example: a new lead fills your form → AI analyzes their response → generates a personalized reply → sends it automatically. This is the future of solo businesses: systems that think and act for you.
learning.models_automation_ex: Workflow: New email → AI categorizes it (support/sales/spam) → If sales: generate personalized response with your offer → Send draft to your inbox for approval. You handle 50 emails in 5 minutes.
learning.models_automation_tip: In Day 6, build at least ONE automation that uses AI. Start simple: 'When I get a new lead → AI writes a follow-up → I review and send.'
learning.prompt_structure_exp: A great prompt has 4 parts: Role (who AI should be), Context (background info), Task (what to do), and Format (how to deliver). The more specific you are, the better the result. Vague prompts = vague answers.
learning.prompt_structure_ex: Weak: 'Write me a bio.' Strong: 'You are a copywriter for tech startups. I'm a solo developer who built a task management app for freelancers. Write a 2-sentence Twitter bio that sounds confident but approachable. Include an emoji.'
learning.prompt_structure_tip: For your next AI Mentor interaction, structure your message as: 'Act as [role]. I need [task]. Context: [details]. Format: [how you want it].'
learning.prompt_role: Role prompting: make AI an expert
learning.prompt_role_exp: When you tell AI to 'act as' a specific expert, it shifts its knowledge and tone. 'Act as a sales coach' gives different advice than 'act as a therapist.' Role prompting is the easiest way to dramatically improve AI output — and it works with every AI tool.
learning.prompt_role_ex: 'Act as a Y Combinator partner reviewing my startup pitch. Be brutally honest. Here's my one-liner: [your offer]. What's weak? What questions would you ask?'
learning.prompt_role_tip: Use role prompting in Day 4: 'Act as my target customer. Read this DM and tell me honestly — would you reply? What would make you ignore it?'
learning.prompt_iteration_exp: Don't expect perfect results from one prompt. The best results come from conversation: generate → evaluate → refine. Say 'make it shorter,' 'add more urgency,' or 'now rewrite it for a different audience.' Each iteration gets you closer to exactly what you need.
learning.prompt_iteration_ex: Round 1: 'Write a cold DM.' Round 2: 'Too formal. Make it sound like a friend texting.' Round 3: 'Good, but add a specific compliment about their recent post.' → Final message is 10x better than the first draft.
learning.prompt_iteration_tip: After every AI response, ask yourself: 'What's 1 thing I'd change?' Then tell AI exactly that. Do 3 rounds minimum for important content in Days 4-5.
learning.prompt_chain: Chain of thought: complex tasks made simple
learning.prompt_chain_exp: For complex tasks, break them into steps and let AI work through each one. Instead of 'Create my marketing strategy,' say 'First, analyze my target audience. Then, suggest 3 channels. Then, write a content plan for the best channel.' This produces dramatically better results.
learning.prompt_chain_ex: 'Step 1: List the top 5 pain points of freelance designers. Step 2: For each pain point, suggest a product idea. Step 3: Score each idea on effort vs. revenue potential. Step 4: Give me a 1-week launch plan for the top-scored idea.'
learning.prompt_chain_tip: For your Day 7 launch, chain your prompts: 'First analyze my product. Then write a launch post. Then suggest 5 communities to share it. Then write a custom intro for each community.'
